title: American Chicken Burger

ingredients.name: boneless chicken, minced or finely chopped

subs.name: minced mutton (keema) for a richer patty; increase cook time by 2 minutes
any locally available burger bun or even thick slices of square bread, pressed lightly
1 tbsp cornflour mixed with 2 tbsp water as a binder and coating
green chutney (coriander-mint) for a desi version; works brilliantly

steps.text: Combine the minced chicken, grated garlic, grated onion, black pepper, chilli powder and salt in a bowl. Mix well for 2 minutes — the mince should start to feel slightly sticky and cohesive.

Divide the mixture into four equal portions and shape each into a flat patty roughly the diameter of your bun. Press a slight dimple in the centre of each patty — this stops them from puffing up during cooking.
Dust each patty lightly with maida on both sides, then dip in the beaten egg and let the excess drip off.
Heat oil in a frying pan or kadai on medium-high flame. Place the patties in a single layer and cook without moving for 4–5 minutes until a golden crust forms on the bottom.
Flip and cook the second side for 4–5 minutes. The patty is done when the juices run clear and the centre feels firm when pressed. Lift onto a plate lined with paper.

Toast the cut sides of the buns in the same pan with a little residual oil until golden, about 1 minute. Assemble with a smear of ketchup on the bottom bun, then cabbage, a tomato slice and the hot patty. Cap and serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → minced mutton (keema) for a richer patty; increase cook time by 2 minutes; bread → any locally available burger bun or even thick slices of square bread, pressed lightly; egg → 1 tbsp cornflour mixed with 2 tbsp water as a binder and coating; tomato-ketchup → green chutney (coriander-mint) for a desi version; works brilliantly
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A kadai or thick-based frying pan works perfectly — maintain medium-high heat so the outside crisps without steaming. Toast buns directly on the tawa for 30 seconds per side. Amul cheese slice can be laid on the patty in the final minute and covered to melt. Sell-by ketchup (Kissan or Maggi) is the ideal condiment.
